name: Optimize Tartalo codegen performance and generated code quality

description: Baseline measurement of current codegen performance

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline metrics before any optimizations

description: Optimize codegen: fast itoa, concatPrologues, writeLine byte writes, escapeForDoubleQuoted prealloc

asi.hypothesis: Reduce allocations in hot paths: replace fmt.Sprintf in tmp() with string concat+fast itoa, use WriteByte instead of WriteString for indentation, preallocate escapeForDoubleQuoted builder, and add concatPrologues to avoid double-slice allocation

description: Re-baseline after reverting needsArgv (no code change)

asi.hypothesis: Re-baseline to confirm metrics after revert
asi.rollback_reason: No code change - just confirming baseline variance is ~100-200ns

description: Remove discard comments and no-op expr stmts to shrink output and reduce codegen work

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating unnecessary comment lines for discarded values reduces strings.Builder work and output size

description: Optimize len() for simple string vars: skip temp, use ${#name} directly

asi.hypothesis: For len(stringVar) we can emit ${#name} directly instead of copying to a temp first, reducing generated code size and runtime overhead

description: Simplify numeric test operands: use $var instead of $((var)) for simple identifiers

asi.hypothesis: In [test -eq] contexts, simple identifiers can use '$name' instead of '$((name))', reducing output size and shell parsing overhead

description: Inline constant range end bounds: skip temp variable for integer literal ranges

asi.hypothesis: Constant integer range ends don't need a temp variable; inlining saves an assignment and a variable declaration

description: Skip cond temp for simple bool/number identifiers in compileCond fallback

asi.hypothesis: Simple boolean/number identifiers in condition fallback don't need a temp; test $var directly

description: Inline bare identifier range ends: skip temp for simple param/variable bounds

asi.hypothesis: For range loops with identifier end bounds like 0..n, skip the __end temp and compare against $n directly

description: Replace fmt.Sprintf with string concat in emitFunc param bindings

asi.hypothesis: fmt.Sprintf has noticeable overhead for simple string assembly; using concat+itoa in hot emitFunc path reduces allocations and CPU time

description: Skip __ret temp for direct function-call assignments and returns

asi.hypothesis: When a function call is the entire RHS of an assignment/return, __ret already holds the value; skip the __retN temp and assign directly from __ret

description: Skip temp for simple variable iteration in for-in loops

asi.hypothesis: When iterating over a simple string variable, use it directly in the heredoc instead of copying to __linesN temp

description: Use bare $var in emitReturn for simple identifiers instead of $((var))

asi.hypothesis: For simple identifier returns, __ret=$name is shorter and faster than __ret=$((name))
Pre-cached indent strings reduce writeLine overhead
asi.next_action_hint: The slice lookup was not faster than raw byte writes for shallow indent
asi.rollback_reason: Slight regression vs bare byte writes

description: Inline array literals in variable declarations: skip __arrN temp

asi.hypothesis: Array literals assigned directly to variables don't need a temp; inline the body into the declaration

description: Lazy __tt_argv: only emit when args() is actually used

asi.hypothesis: __tt_argv setup is expensive in generated scripts; omit it when args() is never called
asi.tradeoff: Small codegen overhead (+116ns) for significant output size reduction (-113 bytes) and runtime improvement (-8ms)

description: Skip __ret init for functions with explicit returns on all paths

asi.hypothesis: Functions that return on all paths don't need __ret initialization; skip it to shrink output

description: Fast-path simple literals in emitVarDecl: skip compileExpr for int/bool/string

asi.hypothesis: Common literals (int, bool, empty string, simple string) don't need the full compileExpr machinery; emit directly
